note: Day-3 offline reproduction of direct role-FK grounding (t142) and bounded-subset ratio population alignment (t96). Deterministic; no live LLM. Schema-generic (no DB/test hardcoding).

db55_t142.question: How many distinct programs have students advised by instructors from the same department as the program?

direct_advisor.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id=s.program_id JOIN instructors i ON s.advisor_instructor_id=i.instructor_id WHERE p.department_id=i.department_id

event_table.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id=s.program_id JOIN academic_advising aa ON s.student_id=aa.student_id JOIN instructors i ON aa.instructor_id=i.instructor_id WHERE p.department_id=i.department_id

db56_t96.question: For each patient, calculate abnormal lab tests as a percentage of completed lab tests.
db56_t96.incorrect_live_sql: SELECT p.patient_id, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN lt.abnormal_flag IN ('critical','high','low') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL)*100.0/NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN lt.test_status='completed' THEN 1 END),0) AS abnormal_percentage FROM patients p JOIN lab_tests lt ON p.patient_id=lt.patient_id GROUP BY p.patient_id
incorrect_fatal: bounded-subset percentage: the denominator population filter does not also constrain the numerator, so the ratio can exceed 100%
db56_t96.corrected_sql: SELECT patient_id, 100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN abnormal_flag IN ('critical','high','low') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS abnormal_percentage FROM lab_tests WHERE test_status='completed' GROUP BY patient_id
